purpose: Machine-readable integration context for AI systems using niltest. Treat this document as an index; linked documentation and source code are authoritative.

package.name: niltest
package.description: Executable specifications, fixed development mocks, and lightweight checks next to Python functions.

runtime_modes.must_configure_before: Importing a module containing @scenario when selecting test or mock.

modes.production: Safe default. @scenario returns the original function without a niltest runtime wrapper.
modes.test: Execute declared cases against the real implementation.
modes.mock: Return fixed values for calls whose arguments exactly match a declared case.

feature_selection.need: Keep a few small, explanatory examples beside a pure or mostly pure function.
feature_selection.avoid_when: The function is already crowded or the specification needs to stay outside the implementation body.

feature_selection.need: Keep cases outside the function and remove the inline expect branch from the source.
feature_selection.avoid_when: You want examples immediately visible inside a short function body.

feature_selection.need: Integrate representative cases with an established test suite, JUnit XML, or coverage.
feature_selection.avoid_when: You only need a quick standalone check; use niltest run instead.

feature_selection.need: Understand a module's signatures, registered cases, input diagnostics, and mockability without executing cases.
feature_selection.avoid_when: You need to verify implementation behavior; use niltest run or pytest.

common_mistakes.mistake: Configuring test or mock after importing a module containing @scenario.
common_mistakes.why_it_fails: Python evaluates decorators while the module loads, so production behavior may already be selected.
common_mistakes.correct_action: Call configure(mode=Mode.TEST or Mode.MOCK) before the import, or set NILTEST_MODE before Python starts.

common_mistakes.mistake: Expecting a mock result for an input that has no exactly matching given case.
common_mistakes.why_it_fails: Mock mode deliberately falls through to the real implementation when no fixed case matches.
common_mistakes.correct_action: Declare the exact case or make the real implementation safe for the unmatched input.

common_mistakes.mistake: Using a raises case as a development mock.
common_mistakes.why_it_fails: Exception cases are verification-only and never act as fixed mocks.
common_mistakes.correct_action: Use a concrete returns case for a mockable response; keep raises for real-implementation verification.

common_mistakes.mistake: Passing a filesystem path such as src/myapp/specs.py to inspect.
common_mistakes.why_it_fails: inspect accepts Python import paths and imports the module.
common_mistakes.correct_action: Use myapp.specs after making the package importable.

public_api.case_contract: Specify exactly one of returns or raises. match is a regular expression used only with raises.
public_api.pydantic: conforms_to() and case execution can validate and normalize Pydantic-compatible typed inputs when the optional extra is installed. Normal application calls are not transformed.

inspect.contract: Pass an importable Python module path. The command imports the module and lists registered scenarios; it does not execute specification cases.

formats.json: Structured output for tools and CI. --json is a compatibility alias.

pytest.behavior_without_flag: The plugin is inert: it does not change modes or import configured modules.
integration: Each niltest case becomes an independent pytest item.
Standard reports, exit codes, JUnit XML, and coverage are preserved.
